head_preview_lines: Not for US distribution
Commissioned Research – marketing material sponsored by SJF Bank
SJF Bank
Strong beat with high-quality income and cost discipline
SJF Bank reported a strong Q1 26, with good underlying momentum. NII increased 2% q/q,
supported by tailwinds from interest income on excess liquidity placed in bonds. Fee income
is at full thrust at +13% q/q, excluding dividends. Remarkable cost control led to a decrease
of 7% y/y, or 2.5% adjusted for one-off reversals. We expect FY2026 PTP at the upper end of
the guidance range, at DKK797m. We value the share at DKK398-481 (from DKK392-471).
Strong NII and fee income print. NII momentum was supported by solid business volume
growth, with core lending +14% y/y and deposits +10% y/y. Excess liquidity totalling DKK9.5bn
gave NII tailwinds from interest income on bonds. We expect FY2026 NII to be supported by
continued healthy volume growth and margin expansion. Strong fee income (excl. dividends)
development was supported by continued strong growth in DLR-mediated mortgage loans

head_preview_lines: First Reaction Note
Equity Research
Produced by KBC Securities NV (Belgium)
argenx announced the FDA approved a label expansion for Vyvgart and
Vyvgart Hytrulo to include all serotypes of adult patients living with gMG
(anti-AChR-Ab positive, anti-MuSK-Ab positive, anti-LRP4-Ab positive, and
triple seronegative). This makes Vyvgart the only treatment approved for all
gMG serotypes – other FcRns are approved in anti-AChR-Ab and anti-MuSK-
Ab positive, but not in anti-LRP4-Ab positive, and triple seronegative gMG
(approx. 10-15% of gMG patients). We note that there were some questions
regarding the label ahead of the approval given the data in the triple
seronegative subgroup, which have now been cleared by this broad label,
thereby allowing Vyvgart to address an additional 11K patients in gMG. €

head_preview_lines: BRIEF NEWS
ACS (€137.20, NEUTRAL, TP €113.70): Abertis extends its largest concession in Mexico by 19 years
Abertis has agreed with Mexico's government to extend its FARAC I concession — operated through subsidiary Red de Carreteras
de Occidente (RCO) — by ~19 years, doubling its remaining life from 22 to over 41 years and pushing the expiry to 2067. The
concession covers a 799-km motorway network connecting the El Bajío industrial corridor with Mexico City and Guadalajara; it
generated €638 million in revenues in 2025, representing 88% of Abertis’s revenues in Mexico. In exchange, Abertis commits to
a €1.2 billion investment plan over 2026–2029 covering capacity and safety improvements, funded at the RCO level through its
own cash flows and debt. The deal also includes an additional tariff increase to be phased in over four years and fixed for the full
remaining term. This is the third extension of FARAC I, following similar agreements in 2014 and 2020.
Our view. Mexico accounts for ~12% of Abertis’s revenues and ~14% of EBITDA. We view the extension of its largest concession
in the country as positive: it extends the average maturity of the concession portfolio and, although Abertis has committed to invest
up to €1.2bn, the deal also secures a tariff increase phased in over the next four years and fixed for the remaining concession
term.
Álvaro Bernal, +34 91 550 87 15, abernal@alantraequities.com
